name: Castell de Cardona - Circular gravel in the Pla del Bages
name_original: Castell de Cardona - Circular gravel por el Pla del Bages
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Sant Mateu de Bages traverse a landscape of rolling hills and extensive Mediterranean forests in the Bages region of Catalonia. The area is characterized by a mosaic of vineyards, olive groves, and cereal fields, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The prominent Serra de Castelltallat, a protected natural area, provides elevated sections and scenic backdrops. Water features like the Riera de Navàs and various streams also crisscross the region. Best touring cycling routes around Sant Mateu De Bages The most popular touring cycling route is Castell de Cardona - Circular gravel in the Pla del Bages, a 37.3 miles (59.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete, featuring gravel sections through the Pla del Bages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Castillo de Boixadors - loop in the Castelltallat Sierra Protected Natural Area, a difficult 22.9 miles (36.8 km) path that winds through the protected natural area of Castelltallat Sierra. Local touring cyclists also love the Sant Sadurní Church of Callús – Totem Poles of Sant Joan loop from FGC Súria (Mercaderies), a 38.8 miles (62.4 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and rolling hills, often completed in about 4 hours 0 minutes. Touring cycling around Sant Mateu de Bages is defined by rolling hills, Mediterranean forests, and expansive vineyards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Sant Mateu de Bages are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Mateu de Bages's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Sant Mateu de Bages?
A: Touring cycling in Sant Mateu de Bages offers a varied landscape. You'll encounter rolling hills, extensive Mediterranean forests, and a mosaic of vineyards, olive groves, and cereal fields. Some routes feature significant elevation gains and unpaved segments, particularly in areas like the Serra de Castelltallat, while others are on mostly paved surfaces.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Sant Mateu de Bages?
A: Yes, Sant Mateu de Bages caters to various skill levels. While many routes are classified as difficult, there are 14 easy routes and 53 moderate routes available, providing options for beginners or families looking for less challenging rides. These routes often wind through agricultural landscapes and offer a gentler experience.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Sant Mateu de Bages?
A: There are over 250 touring cycling routes in Sant Mateu de Bages documented on komoot, offering a wide range of options for exploration.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on touring cycling routes in Sant Mateu de Bages?
A: You'll cycle through diverse natural settings, including pine, holm oak, and kermes oak forests. The prominent Serra de Castelltallat provides elevated sections and scenic backdrops. Water features like the Riera de Navàs and streams of Salo and Coaner also crisscross the terrain, sometimes featuring riparian forests. For panoramic views, the 543-meter Collbaix peak is a notable vantage point.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or cultural sites to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can explore sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelltallat Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the tranquil Sanctuary of Mare de Déu de la Serra, and numerous historic farmhouses (masías). The area is also known for its 'tinas de piedra seca' (dry stone vats), showcasing traditional viticultural architecture. Nearby, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3826689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont Vell de Manresa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Sant Mateu de Bages?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Sant Mateu de Bages are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975675682/castell-de-cardona-circular-gravel-in-the-pla-del-bages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Cardona - Circular gravel in the Pla del Bages&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a circular journey through varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sant Mateu de Bages?
A: Sant Mateu de Bages is suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling through the vineyards and forests. Summers can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though some higher elevation areas might be cooler.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Sant Mateu de Bages?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rolling hills and Mediterranean forests to expansive vineyards, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels. The blend of natural beauty and historical sites is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any specific routes that pass through protected natural areas?
A: Yes, some routes specifically traverse protected natural areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974493115/castillo-de-boixadors-loop-in-the-castelltallat-sierra-protected-natural-area" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Boixadors - loop in the Castelltallat Sierra Protected Natural Area&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the Castelltallat Sierra, which is recognized as a Plan of Spaces of Natural Interest (PEIN) and part of the Natura 2000 network.

Q: Are there any routes that highlight the region's agricultural heritage?
A: Many routes in Sant Mateu de Bages showcase the region's agricultural heritage. You'll frequently cycle through vineyards, olive groves, and cereal fields. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31448764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Sadurní Church of Callús – Totem Poles of Sant Joan loop from FGC Súria (Mercaderies)&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads through agricultural landscapes and rolling hills, offering insights into the local farming traditions.

Q: What are some of the longer touring cycling routes available?
A: For those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33727781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Sadurní Church (Callús) – Bòbila Wetlands loop from FGC Súria (Mercaderies)&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 69.7 km (43.3 miles) and involve significant elevation gain, providing a substantial touring experience.

One of the most important medieval fortresses in Catalonia. It is located on a hill that dominates the saline valley and the Cardener valley, in the municipality of Cardona.

It was built in the year 886 by Wifredo el Velloso. Romanesque and Gothic style,

Cardona is a medieval town with a well-preserved castle complex. In the city center there are numerous restaurants and tapas bars.
Salt also plays an important role in Cardona. You can marvel at mighty salt deposits in the mountain.
More information at https://cardonaturisme.cat/en/things-to-do/cardona-castle/
